extraction.one_line_summary: GROENEWEGEN Dylan won a chaotic sprint after Lotto-NL Jumbo controlled tightly, never allowing breaks more than one minute
tactical_patterns: Lotto-NL Jumbo kept breakaway under one minute throughout, revealing zero risk tolerance for time gaps in flat stages.
DILLIER Silvan targeted intermediate sprint bonuses for GC positioning, scoring six bonus seconds mid-race as deliberate strategy.
QuickStep late attacks inside 10km served as lead-out disruption rather than genuine break attempts, clearing path for JAKOBSEN.

rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: positive: 14th win of season demonstrates consistent top-end sprint speed even after hectic finales
rider_signals.DILLIER Silvan: positive: actively hunting intermediate bonuses for GC after finishing 10th overall in 2017, treating sprints stage tactically
rider_signals.JAKOBSEN Fabio: neutral: five wins in debut season but positioning failed in final 10km despite QuickStep's disruptive efforts
rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: negative: Bora leadout delivered him well but finishing power insufficient against Groenewegen's acceleration
